LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER MORTON MAZAHERI'S, NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITION FOR WRIT IS DENIED. PETITIONER WAS PROPERLY SERVED AND DEFAULTED BY FAILING TO APPEAR. RESPONDENT COMPLIED WITH GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 11520. THE AGENCY TOOK ACTION BASED UPON COMPETENT EXPERT AFFIDAVITS. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Intention To Move For A New Trial
Off Calendar Dec-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PETITIONER MORTON MAZAHERI, MD'S NOTICE OF INTENTION TO MOVE FOR A NEW TRIAL.

APPEARANCES: Albert J. Garcia, Esq. 510/848-5190 for petitioner & moving party; Richard D. Marino, Deputy Atty General 213/897-8644 for respondent & opposing party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED. In addition, the Court ordered that respondent's opposition to be filed nunc pro tunc to 11/04/10. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Intention To Move For A New Trial

Notice Of Intention To Move For A New Trial
Off Calendar Nov-19-2010 Continued To Dec-15-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER MORTON MAZAHERI, M.D.'S NOTICE OF INTENTION TO MOVE FOR A NEW TRIAL. No appearances on this matter. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: continued to December 15, 2010 to be heard by Judge Woolard. Additionally, Respondent needs to file its opposition with the court and provide a courtesy copy to Department 302. The Register of Actions does not reflect that the opposition was filed. (302/PHA) Judge: Paul H. Alvarado, Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR #5797

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
Off Calendar Jun-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER MORTON MAZAHERI'S, Notice of Hearing on Petition for Writ of Administrative Mandate. Appearances: Richard D. Marino, Deputy Attorney General, Department of Justice, (213) 897-8644, appearing for Respondent and opposing party; Albert J. Garcia, Law Offices of Albert J. Garcia, (510) 848-5190, appearing for Petitioner and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Petition for Writ is denied. Petitioner was properly served and defaulted by failing to appear. Respondent complied with Government Code section 11520. The agency took action based upon competent expert affidavits. (302/CWW/PB)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
